content.claim: A UN expert review linked Hungary's growing SEN cohorts to inadequate specialist support: 'the growing number of children with special education needs has not been matched by adequate teacher training or specialist support in Hungary; bigger classes in public schools pose significant challenges to accommodate SEN students and the shortage of specialists, such as doctors and sign language interpreters, further adds to the difficulties.'
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: UN review does not distinguish between capacity gap driven by rising SEN identification versus pre-existing underinvestment
Does not quantify specialist shortfall or provide counterfactual estimate of adequate specialist provision
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
